title: Resources for Small Businesses
text:
MDA Announces Its 2024-2027 MPP Broad Agency Announcement
The Missile Defense Agency (MDA) has released its 2024-2027 Mentor Protégé Program (MPP) Broad Agency Announcement (BAA). The BAA is intended to solicit eligible major Department of Defense (DoD) contractors (Mentor Firms) and eligible small business concerns (Protégé Firms) for participation in the DoD MPP in accordance with Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) 219.71, MentorProtégé Program and DFARS Appendix I, Policy and Procedures for the DoD Mentor Protégé Program
This is a Two-Step Open BAA issued under the provisions of FAR 6.102(d) (2), which provides for the competitive selection of research proposals. Step-One is the submission of white papers. MDA will contact those offerors whose white papers are selected to proceed to Step-Two, submission of a formal proposal, subject to availability of program funds. Formal proposals are not being accepted at this time. DO NOT submit a formal proposal until requested by the MDA Contracting Office.
The MDA MPP BAA is posted to the Contract Opportunities website https://sam.gov/(external link opens in new window)
Go to the announcement on SAM.gov(external link opens in new window) for more information and instructions for how to submit an application.
If you have questions about the MDA Mentor Protégé Program please send an email to mpp@mda.mil.
If you have specific questions about the BAA please send an email to mppbaa@mda.mil.
References
Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) (external link opens in new window)
Defense Federal Acquisition Regulations (DFARs), Subpart 219.71 (external link opens in new window)
DFARS Appendix I (external link opens in new window)
MPP History
The Department's Mentor-Protégé Program (MPP) was established November 5, 1990 (Public Law 101-510). The MPP has been periodically reauthorized by Congress since then, and was made permanent under the Fiscal Year 2023 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA). The purpose of the MPP is to respond to concerns raised by DoD prime contractors that many Small Disadvantaged Businesses do not possess the technical capabilities to perform DoD subcontract requirements, making it difficult for these prime contractors to achieve their SDB subcontracting goals.
The MPP provides incentives to major DoD contractors (Mentor Firms) to furnish eligible small business concerns (Protégé Firms) with engineering, technology and business development assistance to enhance the capabilities of the Protégé Firm to perform as a competitive prime contractor, subcontractor and/or supplier
For more information about the DoD MPP go to following link and MPP brochure.
https://business.defense.gov/Programs/Mentor-Protege-Program(external link opens in new window)
MPP Brochure
Department of Defense (DoD) Nunn Perry Award
The prestigious Nunn-Perry Award, named in honor of former Senator Sam Nunn and former Secretary of Defense William Perry, was first awarded in 1995 to recognize outstanding Mentor-Protégé teams formed under the auspices of the Department of Defense (DoD) Mentor-Protégé program.
Eligibility Requirements:
• Any DoD Mentor Protégé team with a Mentor Protégé agreement that:
• Was approved by a cognizant DoD Component Director, Small Business Programs (e.g., Military Department or Defense Agency);
• Has a performance period of 12 months or greater (agreement requires having at least one (1) completed and approved DCMA Semi-Annual Report (SAR) and one (1) completed and approved DCMA Annual Performance Report);
• Has not previously won a Nunn-Perry Award (under no circumstances, will a Mentor-Protégé team be selected for an award if the nominated agreement has previously received recognition); and
• Submits a nomination on time, and in accordance with all directions in the Call for Nominations.
Nomination Procedures:
Nominations will be accepted from any of the following:
• Mentors
• Protégés
